scenario.context: Station 3 of the daily run: distinguish laws, standards, and baseline protection.
scenario.mission: You classify the legal framework, security policy, and BSI baseline protection for a work area in a practical way.

station.profile: Security policy, rules, and structured implementation frameworks

customerRequest.subject: Station 03: explain the security framework in operations
customerRequest.body: Please prepare a compact classification of laws, standards, security policy, and BSI baseline protection for the work area.

questions.title: Mandatory requirements and implementation framework
questions.prompt: Which statement is technically most accurate?

options.text: Laws are mandatory, standards and baseline protection help with structured implementation
options.rationale: This cleanly separates legal framework and implementation support.

options.text: Laws and standards are always the same
Only internal notes are relevant for security
options.rationale: External requirements must not be ignored.

feedback.correct: Correct: This helps classify the framework in daily work.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: separate legal framework and standards more clearly.

questions.title: Elements of a security framework
questions.prompt: Which points belong in a traceable security framework? (Multiple selections)

options.text: A security policy with clear principles
options.rationale: It provides orientation for further steps.

options.text: Rules or standards for concrete implementation
Documented derivation of measures and responsibilities
options.rationale: Without derivation, the framework remains abstract.

options.text: Only spontaneous individual decisions without guidelines
options.rationale: That does not lead to a solid security framework.

options.text: Complete separation of business unit and IT

feedback.correct: Correct: Policy, rules, and responsibilities fit together sensibly.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: connect policy, implementation rules, and responsibilities more clearly.

questions.title: From policy to implementation
items: Name concrete measures and responsible parties
Capture security policy and framework requirements
Assess protection objects and protection need
Document results in line with the process
correctOrder: Capture security policy and framework requirements
Assess protection objects and protection need
Name concrete measures and responsible parties
Document results in line with the process

feedback.correct: Correct: The security process moves cleanly from requirements to concrete steps.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: go from framework to assessment to prioritization and documentation.

questions.title: Implementation rate from a checklist
questions.prompt: Out of 24 required security controls, 18 are already implemented. What is the implementation rate in percent?

feedback.wrong: Divide implemented controls by the total and convert to percent.

questions.title: Briefly explain the security framework
questions.prompt: Explain in 4-6 sentences how policy, baseline protection, and standards work together in operations.
questions.expectedOutline: Role of the policy, benefit of structured standards, and a brief operational link.

feedback.correct: Correct: The framework is understandable and operationally tangible.
feedback.wrong: Please separate and connect policy, baseline protection, and standards more clearly.
